content:
XSS, CSRF, prototype pollution, injection — real vulnerabilities with real prevention code for every single one.

4 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Security is no longer just the responsibility of security engineers.
Whether you’re building a simple portfolio, an e-commerce platform, or the next SaaS startup, every line of JavaScript you write can either protect your users — or expose them to attackers.
Most developers spend years mastering frameworks like React, Next.js, Vue, or Node.js. Yet surprisingly few understand the vulnerabilities that attackers actively search for every day.
The scary part?
Many security flaws aren’t caused by advanced hacking techniques. They’re introduced by ordinary code that seems perfectly harmless.
In this article, we’ll explore eight JavaScript security vulnerabilities that every developer should know. For each one, you’ll learn:

• Why it happens

• How attackers exploit it

• Vulnerable code

• Secure code

• Best practices
